source: Identification Act 68 of 1997, Department of Home Affairs

segments.name: date_of_birth
segments.description: Date of birth encoded as YYMMDD. YY: 00-99 maps to 1900-1999 or 2000-2099 based on context.
segments.validation: Must be a valid calendar date. Month 01-12, Day 01-31 (per month rules).

segments.name: gender_sequence
segments.description: Gender and sequence number. 0000-4999 = Female, 5000-9999 = Male.

segments.name: obsolete_race_digit
segments.description: Previously used for race classification until 1980. Now typically 8 or 9.
segments.validation: Must be 0-9 (no specific validation, historical artifact).

segments.name: checksum
segments.validation: Must satisfy Luhn checksum on the full 13-digit number.

luhn_algorithm.description: Validate the 13-digit ID number using the Luhn (mod 10) algorithm
steps: 1. Starting from the rightmost digit (position 13), double every second digit (positions 12, 10, 8, 6, 4, 2)
2. If doubling produces a number >= 10, subtract 9 from the result
3. Sum all digits (both doubled and non-doubled)
4. If the total modulo 10 equals 0, the ID number is valid
luhn_algorithm.alternative_description: Concatenate all odd-position digits (1,3,5,7,9,11,13). Concatenate all even-position digits (2,4,6,8,10,12) into a number, multiply by 2, then sum all individual digits of the result. Add to sum of odd-position digits. Result mod 10 must equal 0.
luhn_algorithm.note: South Africa uses a variant where the check digit is calculated such that the total Luhn sum of all 13 digits is divisible by 10.

additional_validations: Date of birth must be a valid calendar date
Person must not have a future date of birth
If extracting age for ETI eligibility: use YYMMDD to derive full date, compare against current date

year_disambiguation.rule: YY values 00-current_YY are treated as 20XX; values above current_YY are treated as 19XX
year_disambiguation.example: In 2026: YY=26 means 2026, YY=27 means 1927, YY=99 means 1999, YY=00 means 2000
